pi_items.source: task.json description states 'Optimize...for Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) of 2.0'; verifier.py line 66
metadata value matched against verifier.py line 67 usage; tolerance of ±0.2 around TSR 2.0 is standard engineering practice
task.json description explicitly states 'create a 12-bladed rotor'; setup_task.sh creates directories for rotor project
task.json description states 'TSR Sweep from 0.5 to 4.0, step 0.1'; verifier.py line 139 checks this range
task.json description line 5 specifies exact path; export_result.sh line 10 defines PERF_FILE; setup_task.sh line 8 removes old file
task.json description line 6 specifies exact path; export_result.sh line 11 defines PROJ_FILE; setup_task.sh line 10 removes old file

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims verified as consistent with task specifications. The task uses QBlade, a real wind turbine simulation software, to generate synthetic data. Key physics parameters are correct: TSR of 2.0 (low TSR regime appropriate for high-solidity pump rotors), 12-bladed configuration (characteristic of high-solidity designs), and TSR sweep from 0.5 to 4.0 (standard optimization range). The NACA 4415 airfoil is a well-documented, widely-used profile in wind turbine design. All output file paths exactly match the expected locations verified in both setup and export scripts.
